name: line-limit
description: Enforce file line count limits (200 recommended, 300 max) for CODE IMPLEMENTATION files only. Use this when reviewing code, creating files, or when files exceed line limits and need modularization.
capabilities: Counts lines in code files automatically
Flags files exceeding 300 lines as violations
Suggests modularization patterns by file type
Creates organized directory structures for splits
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Contains only markdown files describing line limit enforcement and modularization patterns. All 68 static findings are false positives: the 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code block syntax, 'weak cryptographic algorithm' references are JSON line numbers, and 'hardcoded URL' is repository metadata. No security risks present.
valueStatement: Long code files are hard to maintain and understand. This skill automatically checks line counts and helps you split oversized files into manageable, well-organized modules. Keep your codebase clean and navigable.
limitations: Applies only to code implementation files
Excludes documentation, config files, and test fixtures
Cannot split logically coupled code without guidance

useCases.title: Code Review Checks
useCases.description: Automatically flag oversized files during reviews and suggest proper modularization strategies.

useCases.title: Enforce Project Standards
useCases.description: Maintain consistent file size limits across your codebase to improve readability and maintainability.

useCases.title: Learn Modularization
useCases.description: Get guided assistance on how to properly structure and organize large code files step by step.

promptTemplates.title: Basic File Check
promptTemplates.scenario: Check if a file needs splitting
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the line count of src/components/Dashboard.tsx and tell me if it needs modularization

promptTemplates.title: Review with Limits
promptTemplates.scenario: Code review with line limit check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code file and check if it exceeds the recommended line limits: src/utils/helpers.js

promptTemplates.title: Modularize Request
promptTemplates.prompt: This file is too long at 450 lines. Please modularize src/services/api.ts following best practices

promptTemplates.title: New File Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new React component but keep it under 200 lines by splitting into logical sub-components

bestPractices: Keep files under 200 lines for optimal readability and maintenance
Group related functionality together when splitting files
Use consistent naming conventions across split modules
Maintain clear import and export relationships between modules
antiPatterns: Splitting files purely to meet line limits without logical separation
Creating circular dependencies between split modules
Over-fragmenting into single-function files that hurt discoverability
Ignoring code cohesion just to reduce line count

faq.question: Which files are exempt from line limits?
faq.answer: Documentation files, config files, test fixtures, Jupyter notebooks, CSS files, and auto-generated files are exempt from line limits.

faq.question: What happens at exactly 300 lines?
faq.answer: Files at 300 lines trigger a warning. Files with 301 or more lines require mandatory modularization before continuing.

faq.question: Can I customize the line limits?
faq.answer: The skill uses fixed limits of 200 recommended and 300 maximum lines based on established software engineering best practices.

faq.question: Will this modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: No, the skill suggests modularization plans but requires your explicit approval before making any code changes.

faq.question: How does it handle existing import statements?
faq.answer: The skill preserves and properly re-exports all imports when splitting files into multiple modules.

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: Supports major languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, C, C++, and other source code files.
